sessions.goal: Implement WebSocket server for real-time task updates
accomplished: Set up Socket.io server with Next.js custom server
Created WebSocket connection handling in lib/socket.ts
Added initial event handlers for task updates

issues_encountered.issue: Socket.io CORS errors when connecting from frontend
issues_encountered.resolution: Added CORS configuration with explicit origin whitelist

issues_encountered.issue: Next.js custom server breaks API routes hot reload
issues_encountered.resolution: Development mode only uses custom server, production uses standard Next.js

decisions_made.decision: Use Socket.io instead of raw WebSocket API
decisions_made.rationale: Better fallback support, room management, easier reconnection logic

decisions_made.decision: Authenticate WebSocket connections using same JWT as HTTP
decisions_made.rationale: Consistent auth, no separate auth system needed

patterns_learned.lesson: Custom server breaks some Next.js features, use only when necessary (WebSocket)
Must explicitly configure allowed origins, can't use wildcard in production

next_steps: Create client-side React hook for WebSocket connection (useWebSocket)
Implement task update events (emit on create, update, delete)
Add reconnection logic with exponential backoff

b8e4f3a - feat: Add WebSocket authentication middleware
c9d5g4b - feat: Implement connection handling
d0e6h5c - feat: Add task update event handler stubs
e1f7i6d - test: Add WebSocket server tests
f2g8j7e - fix: CORS configuration for Socket.io
g3h9k8f - refactor: Extract socket server to separate file
h4i0l9g - docs: Add WebSocket architecture notes
i5j1m0h - chore: Add Socket.io types
sessions.notes: WebSocket server setup complete. CORS was tricky but resolved. Ready to build client-side integration tomorrow. Bead method working great - 9 commits, always had working code.

sessions.goal: Complete task commenting feature and prepare for real-time updates
accomplished: Added comment UI components with mentions
Added tests for comment functionality (95% coverage)
Completed Story: Task commenting and mentions (5 points)
Researched WebSocket options for next feature

issues_encountered.issue: Mention autocomplete performance slow with 100+ users
issues_encountered.resolution: Added debouncing (300ms) and limit to 10 results

decisions_made.decision: Use @username syntax for mentions instead of complex UI
decisions_made.rationale: Simpler to implement, familiar to users (like GitHub, Twitter)

decisions_made.decision: Store mentions in separate table, not parsed from comment text

patterns_learned.lesson: Always debounce autocomplete, limit results, virtualize lists if >50 items

next_steps: Start real-time task updates feature (next sprint story)

sessions.notes: Comment feature complete! Mentions working well. Ready to start real-time updates. Performance optimization on autocomplete was worth the time.

sessions.goal: Sprint 3 planning and start task commenting feature
accomplished: Calculated velocity: 20 points (avg of last 3 sprints)
Created task breakdown for each story

decisions_made.rationale: Need bi-directional communication, Socket.io provides fallbacks and room management

sessions.notes: Sprint 3 off to good start. Planning went smoothly. Velocity stable at 20 points. Excited about real-time features.

accomplished: Completed Sprint 2 review - demoed all features

patterns_learned.lesson: When integrating with external APIs (Stripe, SendGrid), add 3x buffer to estimates
Schema migration + data migration should be separate beads for safety

z2a8d7y - docs: Update memory files with Sprint 2 learnings
sessions.notes: Great sprint! Exceeded velocity (22 vs 20 expected). Team (me) healthy. Memory system and bead method working perfectly. Ready for Sprint 3.

accomplished: Implemented profile image upload with S3
Added profile bio and social links
Completed Story: User profile customization (3 points)

issues_encountered.issue: Image upload failing silently in production
issues_encountered.resolution: S3 bucket CORS not configured - added allowed origins

decisions_made.decision: Store profile images in S3 instead of database
decisions_made.rationale: Better performance, reduces database size, CDN benefits

patterns_learned.lesson: Must configure CORS in S3 bucket policy for direct uploads from browser

sessions.notes: Profile feature complete! S3 CORS issue cost an hour but learned the pattern. Sprint 2 done - all 22 points completed. Retrospective tomorrow.

sessions.goal: Continue user authentication - add password reset

issues_encountered.issue: Email sending timing out in dev
issues_encountered.resolution: SMTP server misconfigured, switched to Mailtrap for development

decisions_made.decision: Use Mailtrap for development, SendGrid for production
decisions_made.rationale: Mailtrap free for dev testing, SendGrid reliable and affordable for production

patterns_learned.lesson: Always have different email service for dev vs prod. Mailtrap catches all dev emails.

n6o2r1m - fix: Switch to Mailtrap for development

sessions.notes: Password reset working. Email setup was annoying but pattern documented. Almost done with Sprint 2!

accomplished: Finished login endpoint with JWT generation
Built auth middleware for protected routes

decisions_made.decision: Use JWT with httpOnly cookies for auth
decisions_made.rationale: Balance of security (XSS protection) and simplicity (stateless)

patterns_learned.lesson: Always implement token refresh. 15 min access token, 7 day refresh token standard.

sessions.notes: Major feature complete! Auth system solid. JWT refresh was complex but worth it. Bead method helped tremendously - 12 commits, always working. Sprint 2 progressing well.

issues_encountered.issue: Bcrypt rounds too high causing slow registrations
issues_encountered.resolution: Reduced from 12 to 10 rounds (still secure, 2x faster)

decisions_made.rationale: Faster, native ESM support, better Vite integration

patterns_learned.lesson: 10 rounds is secure and fast. Don't over-optimize security at cost of UX.
